vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1970: New-user: minimal New Project flow (add existing / new folder + git init -> register)

plan.title: New-user: minimal New Project flow (add existing / new folder + git init -> register)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Projects can only be registered via `pan project add <existing-path>` in a terminal (src/cli/commands/project.ts:85). That command requires the path to already exist and does not run `git init`. There is no dashboard endpoint to register a project, no server-side folder browser, and no 'create folder + git init' path. A brand-new user has no UI way to get their first project in.
narratives.Proposal: Add a minimal dashboard 'New Project' flow with two modes. (1) Backend: factor the registration core out of projectAddCommand into a server-safe helper (src/lib/project-registration.ts) and add POST /api/projects (mode 'existing' | 'new') in the existing routes/projects.ts — 'new' mode resolves and validates the final target, rejects symlinks/files/non-empty directories/existing repos/home escapes, then does mkdir + async git init before registering. Add a home-clamped GET /api/fs/list-dirs folder browser in a new routes/fs.ts. (2) Frontend: a folder-picker subcomponent, a New Project modal (Add existing | New project toggle, name + parent-folder picker, live path preview, Start -> confirmation -> POST), and wiring of onNewProject() from the sidebar + Home that refetches the project lists and selects the new project. Register only via the single write door (registerProjectSync); git init is async only (PAN-70), requires explicit user confirmation showing the exact canonical target directory, and must never run in an existing repo or symlink-resolved escape; the folder browser is clamped to the user's home.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Setup Agent, templates/archetypes, SetupRpcGroup, or `pan setup` (those are #654 / the full wizard).
- Clone from GitHub/GitLab (a later release).
- Tracker config, quality gates, or workspace-build config in the wizard — use minimal defaults, editable later in projects.yaml.
- Browsing the filesystem outside the user's home directory (the folder browser is home-clamped).
- Polyrepo detection/configuration UI — the CLI's polyrepo next-steps output stays CLI-only; the modal registers a single folder with minimal defaults.
- Changing any `pan project add` CLI flag, help string, or output (the refactor is behavior-preserving / no-loss).

autoDecisions.summary: Folder browser GET /api/fs/list-dirs is hard-clamped to the user's home directory (default start = ~); paths escaping home are rejected (400).
autoDecisions.rationale: Simplest defensible reading of FR-2's loose 'not outside home unless explicitly navigated'; cleanly satisfies AC-4; safe default for a demo. This user's projects live under ~/Projects (within home), so home-clamp covers the demo. Browsing outside home is deferred (NonGoal).

autoDecisions.summary: Shared registration core lives in a new module src/lib/project-registration.ts (registerProjectFromPath), consumed by both projectAddCommand and POST /api/projects.
autoDecisions.rationale: Single-sources the write-door registration core (NFR-2) without bloating projects.ts, and keeps it server-safe (no chalk/console/execSync).

autoDecisions.summary: Registration core keeps the existing sync write door (registerProjectSync) + sync hook symlinks; only `git init` is async.
autoDecisions.rationale: registerProjectSync is the sanctioned single write door (NFR-2) and is sync by design; PAN-70/NFR-1 targets unbounded subprocess blocking, which only git init is.

autoDecisions.summary: 'new' mode rejects (dup key, non-empty target) BEFORE any mkdir/git init.
autoDecisions.rationale: Guarantees no partial write (AC-3): the only filesystem mutations happen after all validation passes.

autoDecisions.summary: `git init` is treated as a potentially dangerous filesystem write: before it runs, the server must validate the final canonical target itself and the UI must show a confirmation dialog with the exact target.
autoDecisions.rationale: Plain git init is usually idempotent in an existing repo, but it still mutates repository metadata. The feature must never run it in the wrong directory, through a symlink escape, in an existing repo, or without explicit user confirmation.

autoDecisions.summary: POST /api/projects added to existing routes/projects.ts; GET /api/fs/list-dirs added as new routes/fs.ts merged into server.ts makeRoutesLayer.
autoDecisions.rationale: projects.ts is the natural home for project registration; filesystem browsing is a distinct concern deserving its own module.

autoDecisions.summary: Per-route auth: POST uses rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest (CSRF), GET uses rejectUnauthorizedDashboardRequest.
autoDecisions.rationale: Auth in this server is per-route, not global (see settings.ts/issues.ts/discovered-sessions.ts). Matching the established gates satisfies 'auth-gated like other routes'.

autoDecisions.summary: PAN-1970 ships a self-contained '+ New Project' trigger; if PAN-1969 already added one, reuse it and avoid duplicates.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps PAN-1970 independently shippable and demoable without hard-depending on PAN-1969 landing first.

autoDecisions.summary: Modal uses the CSS-module overlay/dialog pattern (ForkModal / command-deck.module.css), with the folder picker as a separate subcomponent.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the CommandDeck convention where the New Project flow lives; the split keeps the picker independently testable.

items.title: Extract server-safe registration core (registerProjectFromPath); CLI delegates

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/project-registration.ts exporting `registerProjectFromPath({ path, name? }): { key, config }`. It must: derive key via `name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g,'-')` (name defaults to the folder basename, matching project.ts:97-98); dup-check via getProjectSync (throw a clear duplicate error if already registered — NO second write); build the minimal ProjectConfig { name, path }; call registerProjectSync (the single write door); call ensureProjectLayer; call preTrustDirectory (same symbol projectAddCommand uses today, keep the non-fatal try/catch — H7); and install git hooks only when `.git` is present (move/share the installGitHooks logic from project.ts). The module must contain NO chalk/console and NO execSync (server-safe). Then refactor projectAddCommand (src/cli/commands/project.ts) to delegate its registration core to this helper while keeping ALL existing console output, next-steps guidance, and CLI flags identical (no-loss refactor — D9).

items.title: registerProjectFromPath({path}) registers a project: getProjectSync(key) returns the minimal ProjectConfig {name, path} written through registerProjectSync, and the function returns { key, config }.
Calling registerProjectFromPath with an already-registered key rejects with a clear duplicate error and performs no second projects.yaml write (getProjectSync still returns the original entry).
`pan project add <existing-path>` still produces an identical projects.yaml entry and still prints its next-steps output — existing CLI behavior is preserved (no-loss).
The module src/lib/project-registration.ts contains no `execSync`, no `chalk`, and no `console.` call — it is server-safe.
A unit test under tests/unit/lib/ registers a temp dir, asserts the entry via getProjectSync, and asserts the duplicate path throws without a second write.
POST /api/projects (mode='existing') in routes/projects.ts, CSRF-gated

narrative.Action: Add a `POST /api/projects` route to src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts via HttpRouter.add (merge it into projectsRouteLayer at the bottom). Read the JSON body with the existing readProjectJsonBody pattern (projects.ts:565). Gate it with rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest(request) (CSRF, like settings.ts:811). For body { mode:'existing', path }: validate `path` is a non-empty absolute path that exists (400/404 otherwise); call registerProjectFromPath({ path }); return { key, name, path } as jsonResponse. Map the duplicate error to 409 with a clear message. Wrap in httpHandler.

items.title: POST /api/projects {mode:'existing', path} with a valid existing path returns 200 { key, name, path } and the project is registered (getProjectSync(key) is set).
POST with a missing/nonexistent path returns a 400/404 error and registers nothing.
POST for an already-registered key returns 409 with a clear error and does not mutate projects.yaml.
rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest rejects a request lacking valid dashboard auth/CSRF before any registration occurs.
A route test in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/ covers existing-mode success, missing-path error, and duplicate -> 409 (no write).
Extend POST /api/projects with mode='new' (mkdir + async git init)

narrative.Action: Extend the POST /api/projects handler with mode='new'. For body { mode:'new', parentDir, name }: derive key from name; dup-check and reject (409) BEFORE any fs change; resolve/canonicalize parentDir and compute target = join(canonicalParent, slug(name)); inspect the target itself before any write. If target exists, use lstat/stat/realpath as appropriate and reject symlinks, files, non-empty directories, existing Git repositories (`.git` directory or `.git` file), and any canonical target outside the user's home. If target is missing, create it under the canonical parent, then canonicalize the created directory and re-check the home boundary before `git init`. Only after those checks may the route run `git init` ASYNCHRONOUSLY via `execAsync = promisify(exec)` (like routes/resources.ts:29) or spawn — NEVER execSync (PAN-70); then call registerProjectFromPath({ path: canonicalTarget, name }) and return { key, name, path: canonicalTarget }. Sequencing per D3 plus the git-init safety decision guarantees no partial write and no git init in a dangerous location.

items.title: POST /api/projects {mode:'new', parentDir, name} creates <parentDir>/<slug(name)>, runs git init, registers key=slug(name), and returns 200 { key, name, path } with path === the created folder.
After a successful 'new' create, the target folder contains a .git directory (git init actually ran).
POST 'new' where the target exists and is non-empty returns 409 and makes no filesystem change and no registration (no partial write).
POST 'new' whose derived key duplicates an existing project returns 409 before any mkdir/git init runs (no orphan folder).
A route test covers new-mode success (folder + .git + registered), non-empty-target -> 409 (no fs/registry change), and dup-key -> 409; and asserts no new `execSync` import was added to the route.

Route tests prove `git init` never runs when the target is a symlink to outside home, an existing Git repository (`.git` directory or `.git` file), a regular file, or a non-empty directory; each case returns a clear error with no `.git` creation and no projects.yaml mutation.
GET /api/fs/list-dirs folder browser (new routes/fs.ts), home-clamped + auth-gated

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/routes/fs.ts exporting fsRouteLayer with `GET /api/fs/list-dirs?path=<abs>`. Gate with rejectUnauthorizedDashboardRequest(request) (like discovered-sessions.ts). Resolve the requested path; if absent default to homedir(); CLAMP to home — reject (400) any resolved path that escapes the user's home via `..` or an absolute path outside home (reuse the normalization shape from server.ts:234-246). List only immediate subdirectories with async fs/promises readdir({ withFileTypes:true }), filtering to directories, sorted by name; return jsonResponse({ path, parent, entries: {name,path}[] }) where parent is the containing dir (or null at home root). Import fsRouteLayer and merge it into makeRoutesLayer in server.ts.

items.title: GET /api/fs/list-dirs?path=<dir under home> returns 200 { path, parent, entries } where entries lists only the immediate subdirectories as { name, path }, sorted.
GET /api/fs/list-dirs with no path param defaults to and lists the user's home directory.
A path that resolves outside the user's home (e.g. ../../etc or an absolute /etc) is rejected (400) and never lists a directory outside home.
An unauthenticated request is rejected by rejectUnauthorizedDashboardRequest before any readdir; the route is merged into server.ts makeRoutesLayer.
A route test covers: listing a temp dir under home, default-to-home, and the escape-home rejection (security).
Folder-picker subcomponent consuming GET /api/fs/list-dirs

narrative.Action: Create a FolderPicker React subcomponent (under src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ or a sibling) that fetches GET /api/fs/list-dirs via the cookie-auth fetchWithTimeout helper (src/lib/apiFetch.ts). It renders the current path, an up/parent affordance that navigates to `parent`, and the subdirectory list; clicking a directory refetches with that path; a Select action emits the chosen absolute path through an onSelect(path: string) prop. Follow the pan-style-guide (no pill badges, muted status text). Co-locate a Vitest test under __tests__/.

items.title: FolderPicker renders the subdirectory list and current path from GET /api/fs/list-dirs on mount.
Clicking a directory sends a new list-dirs request for that path and renders its subdirectories; the up affordance opens the returned parent.
Selecting a folder emits the chosen absolute path via the onSelect prop exactly once.
A co-located Vitest test (mocked fetch) covers initial render, navigate-into-dir refetch, and the onSelect emission.
New Project modal (toggle + both modes + path preview + Start -> POST)

narrative.Action: Build the NewProjectModal component using the CSS-module overlay/dialog pattern (matching ForkModal + command-deck.module.css: .forkHelpOverlay/.forkHelpDialog/.forkHeader/.forkFooter). It has a top toggle 'Add existing | New project'. Add existing: embeds the FolderPicker; Start -> POST /api/projects { mode:'existing', path } via fetchWithTimeout. New project: a name field + a parent-folder picker (default parent ~/Overdeck/) + a live preview rendering `<parent>/<slug(name)>` that updates as the name changes; Start opens a confirmation dialog before the POST. The confirmation must show the exact target directory that will be passed to the server for git initialization, the selected parent directory, the derived project key, and plain text that Overdeck will create the folder if needed, run `git init` in that directory, and then register the project. Only the confirmation action sends POST { mode:'new', parentDir, name }; cancel returns to the modal without sending. On a non-OK response, surface the server error message inline and keep the modal open. Expose props for isOpen/onClose and an onCreated(project) callback (used by the wiring bead). Co-locate a Vitest test.

items.title: The modal renders an 'Add existing | New project' toggle; switching the toggle swaps between the folder-picker (existing) form and the name+parent (new) form.
In 'New project' mode the live preview displays `<parent>/<slug(name)>` and updates as the name input changes (e.g. 'My App' -> <parent>/my-app).
Clicking Start sends the correct POST body to /api/projects: { mode:'existing', path } in existing mode and { mode:'new', parentDir, name } in new mode.
When the server returns an error response, the modal displays the error message inline and stays open (no crash, no navigation).
A co-located Vitest test (mocked fetch) covers toggle switching, the new-mode slug preview, and the correct POST body per mode.

In New project mode, clicking Start opens a confirmation dialog that displays the exact target directory, parent directory, derived project key, and git-init/register consequences; cancel sends no request, and confirm sends the POST.
Wire onNewProject() to open the modal; on success refetch lists + select project

narrative.Action: Implement onNewProject() to open NewProjectModal and wire it from the sidebar PROJECTS header (Sidebar.tsx) and the Home Projects section (HomePage.tsx). If PAN-1969 already added a '+ New Project' trigger/stub, replace the stub body to open the modal and do NOT add a duplicate button; if absent, add a minimal '+ New Project' affordance (H1/D7). On NewProjectModal.onCreated: invalidate React Query keys ['command-deck-projects'] and ['registered-projects'] via useQueryClient().invalidateQueries so the new project appears in the sidebar + Home, then select/open it (setSelectedProjectKey(key) + usePanesStore.getState().ensureHome(key)).

items.title: A single '+ New Project' affordance in the sidebar PROJECTS header (and Home Projects section) opens the New Project modal via onNewProject(); no duplicate trigger is rendered if PAN-1969 already added one.
On a successful create the app invalidates/refetches both ['command-deck-projects'] and ['registered-projects'] so the new project appears in the sidebar and Home without a reload.
On a successful create the app opens the new project: it calls setSelectedProjectKey(key) and usePanesStore.ensureHome(key) with the new key.
A Vitest test asserts the success path invalidates both query keys and selects the new project key.
